{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Credit Risk Analyst",
  "description": "Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors&rsquo; first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Credit Risk Analyst

Job Location: Lagos

About the role


	The Risk Management Analyst is responsible for analyzing credit and portfolio data, monitoring risk exposures, and supporting data-driven lending decisions across Moniepoint&rsquo;s ecosystem.&nbsp;
	The role combines credit risk expertise and data analytics to ensure portfolio quality, regulatory compliance, and sustainable growth.


Key Responsibilities
Portfolio Risk Monitoring:


	Track and analyze portfolio performance metrics (PAR, NPL, roll rates, recoveries)
	Monitor delinquency trends (PAR 0, 30, 60, 90, 360)
	Identify early warning signals (EWS) and emerging risk patterns


Data Analysis &amp; Reporting:


	Extract, clean, and analyse large datasets from internal and external systems
	Develop risk dashboards and automated reports
	Provide daily, weekly, and monthly portfolio insights


Credit Decision Support:
Support underwriting with:


	Cash flow and behavioral analysis
	Customer risk profiling
	Provide data-backed recommendations on loan approvals, limits, and pricing


Risk Modeling &amp; Analytics:
Support development of:


	Credit scoring models
	Probability of Default (PD), Loss Given Default (LGD), Exposure at Default (EAD)
	Conduct stress testing and scenario analysis


Portfolio Strategy &amp; Optimization:


	Segment portfolio by industry, geography, and customer type
	Recommend risk-based strategies to optimize growth and minimize losses
	Support collection and recovery strategies using data insights
	Create model for monitoring e.g. Vintage, Credit Risk Appetite, Atypical
	credit model, Exceptions model, IS-WAS Model, Transaction Matrix etc


Regulatory &amp; Compliance Support:


	Support reporting to regulators such as the Central Bank of Nigeria and credit bureau
	Assist with IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss (ECL) calculations
	Ensure alignment with internal policies and risk frameworks


Systems &amp; Process Improvement:


	Improve data pipelines, reporting efficiency, and risk monitoring tools
	Collaborate with product, engineering, and credit teams
	Support automation of risk processes


Key Deliverables:


	Portfolio risk reports (PAR, NPL, vintage analysis)
	Risk dashboards and performance trackers
	Early warning reports
	Stress testing and scenario outputs
	Credit decision insights and recommendations


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Accuracy and timeliness of reports
	Portfolio quality improvement (PAR/NPL reduction)
	Effectiveness of risk insights in decision-making
	Efficiency gains through automation


&nbsp;Experience &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Finance, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics, or related field
	&nbsp;4 &ndash; 5+ years of experience in credit risk, data analytics, or fintech risk roles
	Experience in financial services or fintech is an advantage.


Required Tools &amp; Technologies:
Data &amp; Analytics:


	Microsoft Excel (Advanced)
	SQL
	Python (Pandas, NumPy)
	Visualization &amp; Reporting
	Power BI
	Tableau
	Database &amp; Systems
	MySQL / PostgreSQL
	Core banking/loan systems (e.g., Temenos, Finacle)
	Risk &amp; Statistical Tools
	SAS / R (optional)


Key Skills &amp; Competencies:
Technical Skills:


	Strong data analysis and statistical skills
	SQL and data querying proficiency
	Dashboard and reporting development
	Understanding of risk modeling techniques


Risk Knowledge:


	Credit risk lifecycle and lending processes
	Risk metrics (PAR, NPL, PD, LGD, EAD)
	Knowledge of IFRS 9


Soft Skills:


	Strong analytical thinking
	Attention to detail
	Communication and storytelling with data
	Stakeholder management.


What we can offer you


	Culture - We put our people first and prioritise the well-being of every team member. We&rsquo;ve built a company where all opinions carry weight and where all voices are heard. We value and respect each other and always look out for one another. Above all, we are human.
	Learning - We have a learning and development-focused environment with an emphasis on knowledge sharing, training, and regular internal technical talks.
	Compensation - You&rsquo;ll receive an attractive salary, pension, health insurance, plus other benefits.


What to expect in the hiring process


	A preliminary phone call with one of our recruiters.
	A panel interview with the member of the team
	An executive interview with the hiring manager

Credit Risk Analyst Job at Moniepoint




        
    

        
        Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors’ first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Credit Risk Analyst

Job Location: Lagos

About the role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Risk Management Analyst is responsible for analyzing credit and portfolio data, monitoring risk exposures, and supporting data-driven lending decisions across Moniepoint’s ecosystem. 
	The role combines credit risk expertise and data analytics to ensure portfolio quality, regulatory compliance, and sustainable growth.


Key Responsibilities
Portfolio Risk Monitoring:


	Track and analyze portfolio performance metrics (PAR, NPL, roll rates, recoveries)
	Monitor delinquency trends (PAR 0, 30, 60, 90, 360)
	Identify early warning signals (EWS) and emerging risk patterns


Data Analysis & Reporting:


	Extract, clean, and analyse large datasets from internal and external systems
	Develop risk dashboards and automated reports
	Provide daily, weekly, and monthly portfolio insights


Credit Decision Support:
Support underwriting with:


	Cash flow and behavioral analysis
	Customer risk profiling
	Provide data-backed recommendations on loan approvals, limits, and pricing


Risk Modeling & Analytics:
Support development of:


	Credit scoring models
	Probability of Default (PD), Loss Given Default (LGD), Exposure at Default (EAD)
	Conduct stress testing and scenario analysis


Portfolio Strategy & Optimization:


	Segment portfolio by industry, geography, and customer type
	Recommend risk-based strategies to optimize growth and minimize losses
	Support collection and recovery strategies using data insights
	Create model for monitoring e.g. Vintage, Credit Risk Appetite, Atypical
	credit model, Exceptions model, IS-WAS Model, Transaction Matrix etc


Regulatory & Compliance Support:


	Support reporting to regulators such as the Central Bank of Nigeria and credit bureau
	Assist with IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss (ECL) calculations
	Ensure alignment with internal policies and risk frameworks


Systems & Process Improvement:


	Improve data pipelines, reporting efficiency, and risk monitoring tools
	Collaborate with product, engineering, and credit teams
	Support automation of risk processes


Key Deliverables:


	Portfolio risk reports (PAR, NPL, vintage analysis)
	Risk dashboards and performance trackers
	Early warning reports
	Stress testing and scenario outputs
	Credit decision insights and recommendations


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Accuracy and timeliness of reports
	Portfolio quality improvement (PAR/NPL reduction)
	Effectiveness of risk insights in decision-making
	Efficiency gains through automation


 Experience & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics, or related field
	 4 – 5+ years of experience in credit risk, data analytics, or fintech risk roles
	Experience in financial services or fintech is an advantage.


Required Tools & Technologies:
Data & Analytics:


	Microsoft Excel (Advanced)
	SQL
	Python (Pandas, NumPy)
	Visualization & Reporting
	Power BI
	Tableau
	Database & Systems
	MySQL / PostgreSQL
	Core banking/loan systems (e.g., Temenos, Finacle)
	Risk & Statistical Tools
	SAS / R (optional)


Key Skills & Competencies:
Technical Skills:


	Strong data analysis and statistical skills
	SQL and data querying proficiency
	Dashboard and reporting development
	Understanding of risk modeling techniques


Risk Knowledge:


	Credit risk lifecycle and lending processes
	Risk metrics (PAR, NPL, PD, LGD, EAD)
	Knowledge of IFRS 9


Soft Skills:


	Strong analytical thinking
	Attention to detail
	Communication and storytelling with data
	Stakeholder management.


What we can offer you


	Culture - We put our people first and prioritise the well-being of every team member. We’ve built a company where all opinions carry weight and where all voices are heard. We value and respect each other and always look out for one another. Above all, we are human.
	Learning - We have a learning and development-focused environment with an emphasis on knowledge sharing, training, and regular internal technical talks.
	Compensation - You’ll receive an attractive salary, pension, health insurance, plus other benefits.


What to expect in the hiring process


	A preliminary phone call with one of our recruiters.
	A panel interview with the member of the team
	An executive interview with the hiring manager
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Electrical Engineer",
  "description": "Oilserv Limited is a leading provider of integrated engineering, procurement, construction, installation &amp; commissioning (EPCIC) services and complementary solutions across the asset and program life cycle within the onshore, offshore and subsea market segments of the oil and gas sector.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Engineer

Job Location: Rivers
Job Type: Full Time

Job Summary


	Designing power distribution systems, lighting systems, grounding systems, and control systems for oil and gas facilities.
	Developing electrical single-line diagrams, schematics, and wiring diagrams.


Job Details
Electrical System Design:


	Performing load calculations and voltage drop analyses.
	Specifying electrical equipment, such as transformers, switchgear, motors, and cables.


Safety and Compliance:


	Ensuring that all electrical designs and installations comply with relevant industry standards (e.g., IEEE, IEC, NEC, API) and safety regulations.
	Conducting hazardous area classifications and designing electrical systems for safe operation in explosive environments.
	Participating in safety studies, such as HAZOP and risk assessments.


Project Execution:


	Preparing technical specifications and bid documents for electrical equipment and materials.
	Evaluating vendor proposals and providing technical recommendations.
	Overseeing the installation, testing, and commissioning of electrical systems.
	Providing technical support during construction and start-up phases.
	Reviewing vendors documents.


Technical Documentation:


	Preparing and maintaining accurate electrical drawings, documents, and reports.
	Developing operating and maintenance procedures for electrical systems.


Collaboration and Coordination:


	Collaborating with other engineering disciplines (e.g., process, mechanical, instrumentation) to ensure integrated designs.
	Coordinating with vendors, contractors, and clients.
	Communicating technical information effectively.


Key Aspects in the Oil and Gas Context:
Hazardous Environments:


	A significant focus on designing and implementing electrical systems that are safe for operation in potentially explosive atmospheres.


Large-Scale Projects:


	Involvement in complex, large-scale projects that require careful planning and coordination.


Remote Locations:


	Potential for working on projects in remote or offshore locations, which may present unique challenges.


Requirements


	A Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) or Bachelor of Engineering (B.Eng.) in Electrical Engineering or related field.
	A Master&#39;s degree (M.Sc.) will be an added advantage
	Registered engineer with COREN is mandatory&nbsp;
	Extensive Industry Track Record:
	
		A minimum of 7-10+ years of relevant experience is commonly expected.
		Proven experience in large-scale EPC projects within the oil and gas industry is paramount.
		Experience with both onshore and offshore projects is highly desirable.
	
	
	Leadership and Mentorship:
	
		Ability to lead electrical engineering teams and provide technical guidance.
		Experience in mentoring junior engineers.
	
	
	Project Lifecycle Expertise:
	
		Deep understanding of all project phases, from feasibility studies and FEED to detailed design, construction, commissioning, and start-up.
		Experience in managing and coordinating electrical engineering activities throughout the project lifecycle.
	
	


Benefits


	Great work culture and multiple opportunities for growth and self-development.&nbsp;
	18 days of annual leave, excluding federal public holidays.&nbsp;
	Health insurance coverage.&nbsp;
	Monthly employer pension contribution.&nbsp;
",

Electrical Engineer Job at Oilserv Limited




        
    

        
        Oilserv Limited is a leading provider of integrated engineering, procurement, construction, installation & commissioning (EPCIC) services and complementary solutions across the asset and program life cycle within the onshore, offshore and subsea market segments of the oil and gas sector.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Engineer

Job Location: Rivers
Job Type: Full Time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Designing power distribution systems, lighting systems, grounding systems, and control systems for oil and gas facilities.
	Developing electrical single-line diagrams, schematics, and wiring diagrams.


Job Details
Electrical System Design:


	Performing load calculations and voltage drop analyses.
	Specifying electrical equipment, such as transformers, switchgear, motors, and cables.


Safety and Compliance:


	Ensuring that all electrical designs and installations comply with relevant industry standards (e.g., IEEE, IEC, NEC, API) and safety regulations.
	Conducting hazardous area classifications and designing electrical systems for safe operation in explosive environments.
	Participating in safety studies, such as HAZOP and risk assessments.


Project Execution:


	Preparing technical specifications and bid documents for electrical equipment and materials.
	Evaluating vendor proposals and providing technical recommendations.
	Overseeing the installation, testing, and commissioning of electrical systems.
	Providing technical support during construction and start-up phases.
	Reviewing vendors documents.


Technical Documentation:


	Preparing and maintaining accurate electrical drawings, documents, and reports.
	Developing operating and maintenance procedures for electrical systems.


Collaboration and Coordination:


	Collaborating with other engineering disciplines (e.g., process, mechanical, instrumentation) to ensure integrated designs.
	Coordinating with vendors, contractors, and clients.
	Communicating technical information effectively.


Key Aspects in the Oil and Gas Context:
Hazardous Environments:


	A significant focus on designing and implementing electrical systems that are safe for operation in potentially explosive atmospheres.


Large-Scale Projects:


	Involvement in complex, large-scale projects that require careful planning and coordination.


Remote Locations:


	Potential for working on projects in remote or offshore locations, which may present unique challenges.


Requirements


	A Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) or Bachelor of Engineering (B.Eng.) in Electrical Engineering or related field.
	A Master's degree (M.Sc.) will be an added advantage
	Registered engineer with COREN is mandatory 
	Extensive Industry Track Record:
	
		A minimum of 7-10+ years of relevant experience is commonly expected.
		Proven experience in large-scale EPC projects within the oil and gas industry is paramount.
		Experience with both onshore and offshore projects is highly desirable.
	
	
	Leadership and Mentorship:
	
		Ability to lead electrical engineering teams and provide technical guidance.
		Experience in mentoring junior engineers.
	
	
	Project Lifecycle Expertise:
	
		Deep understanding of all project phases, from feasibility studies and FEED to detailed design, construction, commissioning, and start-up.
		Experience in managing and coordinating electrical engineering activities throughout the project lifecycle.
	
	


Benefits


	Great work culture and multiple opportunities for growth and self-development. 
	18 days of annual leave, excluding federal public holidays. 
	Health insurance coverage. 
	Monthly employer pension contribution.

  "description": "Workland - Our client is an education and EdTech-focused organization committed to helping learners, educators, and professionals build future-relevant skills through structured learning programs, certifications, and professional development initiatives.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant to the CEO

Job Location: Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	The Executive Assistant serves as the operational extension of the CEO and supports executive coordination, communication management, scheduling, and organisational follow-through.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage CEO&#39;s calendar, scheduling, and meeting coordination
	Track action points and ensure follow-through
	Support internal communication and reporting
	Coordinate strategic engagements and administrative activities
	Prepare briefs, reports, and presentations
	Maintain organized records and documentation systems
	Monitor execution timelines across teams


What Success Looks Like


	Reduction in executive bottlenecks
	Improved coordination efficiency
	Timely follow-through on priorities
	Professional communication quality
	Improved organizational accountability


Ideal Candidate Profile


	3-5 years of experience
	Highly organized and proactive
	Strong written and verbal communication
	Detail-oriented and execution-focused
	Ability to multitask effectively
	Professional maturity and discretion.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: worklandhq@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Application Process


	Application
	Shortlisting
	Assessment
	Interview
	Final Selection.

Executive Assistant to the CEO Job at Workland




        
    

        
        Workland - Our client is an education and EdTech-focused organization committed to helping learners, educators, and professionals build future-relevant skills through structured learning programs, certifications, and professional development initiatives.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant to the CEO

Job Location: Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Executive Assistant serves as the operational extension of the CEO and supports executive coordination, communication management, scheduling, and organisational follow-through.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage CEO's calendar, scheduling, and meeting coordination
	Track action points and ensure follow-through
	Support internal communication and reporting
	Coordinate strategic engagements and administrative activities
	Prepare briefs, reports, and presentations
	Maintain organized records and documentation systems
	Monitor execution timelines across teams


What Success Looks Like


	Reduction in executive bottlenecks
	Improved coordination efficiency
	Timely follow-through on priorities
	Professional communication quality
	Improved organizational accountability


Ideal Candidate Profile


	3-5 years of experience
	Highly organized and proactive
	Strong written and verbal communication
	Detail-oriented and execution-focused
	Ability to multitask effectively
	Professional maturity and discretion.


Method of Application 

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Application Process


	Application
	Shortlisting
	Assessment
	Interview
	Final Selection.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at Smart City
content:
Smart City provides reliable internet service to residential and enterprise customers using fiber optics technology.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Customer Service & Administrative Officer

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive and organized Customer Service & Administrative Officer to manage customer interactions and provide front desk administrative support.
	The ideal candidate will attend to walk-in customers, respond to customer complaints and enquiries through phone calls and emails and whatsapp chats, and ensure smooth day-to-day office communication and coordination.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome and attend to walk-in customers professionally.
	Handle customer complaints, enquiries, and support requests via phone calls, email, and in person.
	Provide accurate information about company services, subscriptions, and processes.
	Follow up with customers to ensure issues are resolved promptly.
	Maintain proper records of customer interactions and complaints.
	Assist with office administrative and front desk duties.
	Coordinate internal communication between departments when resolving customer issues.
	Prepare and manage basic reports, documentation, and correspondence.
	Maintain a clean, organized, and professional reception/work area.
	Support general office operations and other assigned administrative tasks.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess an OND/HND/Bachelor’s Degree in any related field with 0-2 years experience.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to remain calm and professional when handling complaints.
	Basic computer knowledge and familiarity with email and office applications.
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Customer service experience is an added advantage.
	Must reside in Ibadan.


Skills & Qualities:


	Excellent verbal and written communication
	Problem-solving ability
	Professional appearance and attitude
	Attention to detail
	Team player with good work ethics





What We Offer


	Salary: N70,000 - N120,000 / Month.
	Friendly work environment
	Career growth opportunities
	Hands-on experience in the technology and internet service industry


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using "Application for Customer Service & Administrative Officer" as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Inventory & Asset Management Officer

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a reliable and detail-oriented Inventory & Asset Management Officer to oversee the company’s inventory, equipment, and asset records.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for monitoring stock levels, tracking the movement of company assets, maintaining accurate inventory records, and ensuring proper storage and accountability of materials and equipment.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate records of inventory, tools, devices, and company assets.
	Monitor stock levels and notify management when replenishment is required.
	Track the issuance, return, and movement of equipment and materials.
	Conduct routine stock counts and periodic inventory audits.
	Ensure proper labeling, storage, and organization of inventory items.
	Prepare inventory and asset reports for management review.
	Coordinate with procurement and technical teams regarding stock usage and requirements.
	Investigate and report discrepancies, losses, or damaged items.
	Maintain documentation for purchases, transfers, and asset assignments.
	Ensure proper handling and accountability of company property.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND / Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Accounting, or related field.
	Good organizational and record-keeping skills.
	Basic computer proficiency, especially in Microsoft Excel or inventory management software.
	Ability to work with numbers and maintain accurate documentation.
	Previous inventory or storekeeping experience is an added advantage.
	Must reside in Ibadan.


Skills & Qualities:


	Strong attention to detail
	Organizational and multitasking ability
	Integrity and accountability
	Good communication skills
	Ability to work independently and within a team
	Problem-solving and reporting skills.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary
	Professional working environment
	Career growth opportunities
	Exposure to technology and operations management.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Application for Inventory & Asset Management Officer" as the subject of the email.

 


3.) Accounts & Human Resources Officer

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and organized Accounts & Human Resources Officer to oversee daily accounting operations and support human resource administration.
	The ideal candidate will manage financial records, staff documentation, payroll support, and administrative HR processes while ensuring accuracy, compliance, and efficiency in company operations.


Key Responsibilities
Accounting Duties:


	Record and maintain accurate financial transactions and bookkeeping records.
	Prepare invoices, receipts, payment records, and expense reports.
	Monitor company expenses, account balances, and cash flow.
	Assist with payroll processing and salary administration.
	Reconcile bank statements and financial records.
	Prepare periodic financial reports and summaries for management.
	Support tax, pension, and statutory payment documentation.


Human Resources Duties:


	Maintain employee records and HR documentation.
	Assist with recruitment, onboarding, and staff orientation processes.
	Monitor staff attendance, leave records, and performance documentation.
	Coordinate internal staff communication and administrative matters.
	Ensure company policies and procedures are properly implemented.
	Support employee welfare and handle basic HR-related enquiries.


Requirements


	Minimum of HND / Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, Human Resources, or related field.
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles and HR administration.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, especially Excel.
	Strong numerical, organizational, and record-keeping skills.
	Ability to handle confidential information professionally.
	Previous accounting or HR experience is an added advantage.
	Must reside in Ibadan.


Skills & Qualities:


	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Time management and multitasking ability
	Professionalism and integrity
	Problem-solving mindset
	Ability to work independently and within a team.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary; N100,000 - N170,000 monthly. 
	Professional work environment
	Career development opportunities
	Exposure to fast-growing technology and ISP operations.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Application for Accounts & Human Resources Officer" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Tech Operations Manager",
  "description": "International Breweries Plc - Our Dream is to bring people together for a better world. Beer, the original social network, has been bringing people together for thousands of years. We are committed to building great brands that stand the test of time and to brewing the best beers using the finest natural ingredients.

Our diverse portfolio of well over 400 beer brands includes global brands Budweiser, Corona and Stella Artois; multi-country brands Beck&rsquo;s, Castle, Castle Lite, Hoegaarden, and Leffe; and local champions such as Aguila, Bud Light, Jupiler, Klinskoye, Modelo Especial, Quilmes, Skol, and Victoria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Tech Operations Manager

Job ID: 30097959
Job Location: Nigeria
Job Type: Full time

Job Summary


	The key purpose as an IT Operations Manager, you will be at the forefront of our Tech Strategy, ensuring seamless integration and functionality of our systems.
	You will lead a local team of IT professionals and collaborate with regional and global counterparts to implement best practices and innovative solutions.


Key roles and responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day operations of our IT Systems for Nigeria. Including hands-on support from time to time
	Implement and support IT strategies that align with our business goals
	Manage IT projects, ensuring timely completion within budget
	Foster a culture of continuous improvement and innovation within the local systems department
	Ensure compliance with IT standards, policies, and regulations
	Provide leadership and guidance to your team, nurturing talent and encouraging professional growth
	Support the Nigeria management team to align with technology leads in Africa and Global Tech Teams
	Support the execution of local business strategy
	Accountable for availability, stability, and performance of core IT services (end?user, infrastructure, network, applications)
	Own and manage major incidents, root cause analysis, and corrective actions.
	Ensure effective execution of IT service management processes (incident, problem, change)
	Act as the single point of accountability for Tech service delivery and operational performance in Nigeria


Minimum requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field
	At least 6 to 8 years of experience in Tech management operations, preferably in the CPG industry.


Key attributes and competencies:


	Proven track record of managing Tech Operations and managing teams
	Strong project management skills and experience with cross-functional collaboration
	Excellent communication skills and the ability to engage stakeholders at all levels
	Experience with managing Tech suppliers
	Strong problem-solving mindset with the ability to make pragmatic decisions
	Ability to work independently in an ambiguous environment
	Exposure to ERP systems (e.g. SAP, Syspro) in a manufacturing or FMCG environment
	Comfortable operating in resource?constrained and high?pressure environments
	Proven ability to influence without authority in matrixed organisations.


Additional information:


	Band: VI
",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-22",

  },

Tech Operations Manager Job at International Breweries Plc




        
    

        
        International Breweries Plc - Our Dream is to bring people together for a better world. Beer, the original social network, has been bringing people together for thousands of years. We are committed to building great brands that stand the test of time and to brewing the best beers using the finest natural ingredients.

Our diverse portfolio of well over 400 beer brands includes global brands Budweiser, Corona and Stella Artois; multi-country brands Beck’s, Castle, Castle Lite, Hoegaarden, and Leffe; and local champions such as Aguila, Bud Light, Jupiler, Klinskoye, Modelo Especial, Quilmes, Skol, and Victoria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Tech Operations Manager
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job ID: 30097959
Job Location: Nigeria
Job Type: Full time

Job Summary


	The key purpose as an IT Operations Manager, you will be at the forefront of our Tech Strategy, ensuring seamless integration and functionality of our systems.
	You will lead a local team of IT professionals and collaborate with regional and global counterparts to implement best practices and innovative solutions.


Key roles and responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day operations of our IT Systems for Nigeria. Including hands-on support from time to time
	Implement and support IT strategies that align with our business goals
	Manage IT projects, ensuring timely completion within budget
	Foster a culture of continuous improvement and innovation within the local systems department
	Ensure compliance with IT standards, policies, and regulations
	Provide leadership and guidance to your team, nurturing talent and encouraging professional growth
	Support the Nigeria management team to align with technology leads in Africa and Global Tech Teams
	Support the execution of local business strategy
	Accountable for availability, stability, and performance of core IT services (end?user, infrastructure, network, applications)
	Own and manage major incidents, root cause analysis, and corrective actions.
	Ensure effective execution of IT service management processes (incident, problem, change)
	Act as the single point of accountability for Tech service delivery and operational performance in Nigeria


Minimum requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field
	At least 6 to 8 years of experience in Tech management operations, preferably in the CPG industry.


Key attributes and competencies:


	Proven track record of managing Tech Operations and managing teams
	Strong project management skills and experience with cross-functional collaboration
	Excellent communication skills and the ability to engage stakeholders at all levels
	Experience with managing Tech suppliers
	Strong problem-solving mindset with the ability to make pragmatic decisions
	Ability to work independently in an ambiguous environment
	Exposure to ERP systems (e.g. SAP, Syspro) in a manufacturing or FMCG environment
	Comfortable operating in resource?constrained and high?pressure environments
	Proven ability to influence without authority in matrixed organisations.


Additional information:


	Band: VI
